name: Toast
description: Ephemeral, accessible notification messages. Auto-dismiss with pause-on-hover/focus, swipe-to-dismiss on touch, queue stacking, screen-reader announcements (polite by default, urgent for type='error'), and respects prefers-reduced-motion. Imperative API via the useToast() hook. Built on Base UI Toast with StyleX styling.
anatomy:
<Toast.Provider>
  {/* app content; call useToast().add(...) anywhere inside */}
  <Toast.Portal>
    <Toast.Viewport>
      {useToast().toasts.map((t) => (
        <Toast.Root key={t.id} toast={t}>
          <Toast.Content>
            <Toast.Title />
            <Toast.Description />
          </Toast.Content>
          <Toast.Action />
          <Toast.Close />
        </Toast.Root>
      ))}
    </Toast.Viewport>
  </Toast.Portal>
</Toast.Provider>

Provider.description: Owns the toast queue, timers, and announcement state. Mount once near the app root. Renders no DOM element.

limit.description: Maximum number of visible toasts. Older toasts are evicted when exceeded.

toastManager.description: Optional global manager (created via createToastManager) for adding toasts outside React.

Portal.description: Renders the viewport in a React portal outside the DOM tree.

container.description: Target parent element for the portal.

Viewport.description: Container region that holds all rendered toasts. Manages focus (F6) and stacking. Pause-on-hover/focus is wired here.

dataAttributes.data-expanded: Present when the user has expanded the toast stack (focus/hover).

Root.description: Wraps a single toast. Sets role='status' (or 'alert' for high priority), aria-live, swipe gesture, and transition data attributes. Receives the queued ToastObject via the `toast` prop.

toast.description: The toast object yielded by useToast().toasts.map(...).

swipeDirection.description: Direction(s) the toast can be swiped to dismiss.

sx.description: StyleX styles for consumer overrides. Destructive styling auto-applies when toast.type is 'error' or 'destructive'.

dataAttributes.data-expanded: Present when the stack is expanded.
dataAttributes.data-limited: Present when the toast was evicted by `limit`.
dataAttributes.data-swiping: Present while the user is swiping.
dataAttributes.data-type: Mirrors the toast.type string for variant styling.

Content.description: Container for Title and Description. Provides consistent vertical rhythm.

Title.description: Toast heading. Wired automatically to the toast labelling for screen readers.

Action.description: Optional action button (e.g. 'Undo'). Keyboard-reachable via the viewport's F6 hotkey without stealing focus.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

Close.description: Dismisses the toast. Renders an X icon by default with aria-label='Close notification'.

cssRequirements.description: Toast enter/exit fade+slide and swipe-reactive transform live in global CSS. StyleX cannot target Base UI data attributes, so these rules belong in the consumer's global stylesheet inside @layer priority1.

intents.reasoning: Toast is the right component for short-lived, non-blocking feedback after a user action (saved, copied, sent).
intents.composition:
const { add } = useToast();

add({ title: 'Saved', description: 'Your changes were saved.' });

intents.reasoning: type='error' upgrades the announcement to assertive and applies destructive styling without blocking the user.
intents.composition:
const { add } = useToast();

add({ title: 'Couldn’t save', description: 'Try again in a moment.', type: 'error' });

intents.reasoning: Toast.Action provides a keyboard-reachable hotspot (F6 from anywhere) for one-tap reversal of recent changes.
useToast().promise() shows a loading toast that resolves to success or error automatically.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Toasts auto-dismiss and don’t demand acknowledgment. Use AlertDialog for confirmations the user must accept.

avoidWhen.scenario: Persistent or critical errors that block the workflow
avoidWhen.reasoning: Toasts disappear. Use an inline alert or Dialog for blocking errors or required action.

avoidWhen.scenario: Long-form content, forms, or multi-step interactions
avoidWhen.reasoning: Toasts are intentionally compact and ephemeral. Use Dialog or Drawer for substantial content.

avoidWhen.scenario: Information the user must read to continue
avoidWhen.reasoning: Auto-dismiss means the user can miss the message. Use Dialog or inline alert for required reading.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Polite info toast with title and description

examples.name: with-action
destructive
provider-setup
examples.description: Mount the provider, portal, viewport, and a styled root once near the app root
